name: The Cueva del Turche — Circular walk from Turis
name_original: La Cueva del Turche — Paseo circular desde Turís
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Turís offer a diverse landscape for cyclists, characterized by varied terrain including gently rolling hills and areas near the Magre River. The region features a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, suitable for different bike types. Cyclists can explore Mediterranean forests of pine trees and bushes, alongside medieval Valencian farmland, providing a rich natural environment. The area's topography includes moderate elevation changes, making it suitable for a range of fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Turís The most popular touring cycling route is The Cueva del Turche — Circular walk from Turis, a 22.0 miles (35.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural area around the Cueva del Turche. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bike loop from Alborache, a moderate 15.0 miles (24.1 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop through the local countryside, featuring varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Entrance to Cortitxelles – La Vinya Urbanization Park loop from Corral dels Franxos, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail leading through a mix of natural and developed areas, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Turís is defined by varied terrain, rolling hills, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Turís are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 28 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Turís's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Turís?
A: Turís offers a wide selection for touring cyclists, with nearly 150 routes to explore. These routes vary significantly in length, elevation gain, and difficulty, catering to a broad range of cycling preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Turís?
A: The touring cycling routes around Turís feature diverse terrain, including gently rolling hills and areas near the Magre River. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, suitable for road, gravel, and trekking bikes. Cyclists can ride through picturesque Mediterranean forests of pine trees and bushes, as well as medieval Valencian farmland.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Turís offers options for various skill levels. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are 5 easy routes and 55 moderate routes available. These provide a great way to enjoy the scenery without overly challenging climbs, often featuring scenic loops through the local countryside.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Turís?
A: The touring cycling routes in Turís are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 28 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead to stunning natural water features. The region is known for attractions like the Turche Cave Waterfall and the Barranc de Galleç Waterfall. You might also encounter natural pools such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/331737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mañán Pool&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6796199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca de las Palomas&lt;/a&gt;. One popular route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918628238/the-cueva-del-turche-circular-walk-from-turis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cueva del Turche — Circular walk from Turis&lt;/a&gt;, specifically explores the natural area around the Cueva del Turche.

Q: Can I find routes that include natural landmarks or viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer opportunities to visit natural landmarks and enjoy panoramic views. You could cycle towards the Embalse de Forata reservoir for diverse scenic elements, or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4047553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de la Lomiquia&lt;/a&gt;. Historic sites like El Castellet also offer beautiful vistas over agricultural land and the town, accessible via simple trails that cyclists could explore.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Turís?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Turís are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Alborache&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic circular path through the local countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14885146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to Cortitxelles – La Vinya Urbanization Park loop from Corral dels Franxos&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through a mix of natural and developed areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Turís?
A: Turís, being in the Valencian Community of Spain, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures and less intense sun, making for comfortable rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Turís has 89 difficult routes. These often feature significant elevation changes and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Pinar – Road to Valencia loop from Godelleta&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 32 km with substantial climbs.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, Turís and its surrounding towns offer various establishments where you can stop for refreshments. Many routes pass through or near villages, providing opportunities to refuel and rest. It's always a good idea to check local amenities along your chosen route beforehand.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of natural and developed areas?
A: Yes, the region's routes often blend natural landscapes with glimpses of local life. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21956019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Pinar – Restaurante El Canario loop from Turís&lt;/a&gt; takes you through both natural settings and areas with local establishments, offering a varied experience.
tips: The Cueva Turche in Buñol is a beautiful spot worth visiting, especially after heavy rains, when we can enjoy a waterfall that falls 60 metres high.

A wonderful and ideal place to take a break at its picnic tables and benches.
It is an old 18th century waterwheel that was used to raise water from the drought. Today you can see it restored and appreciate some details of its construction in those times that will leave you amazed.
